rationale.D1: Confirmed full per-subsystem IO layer (ElevatorIO, PivotIO, ClimberIO, AlgaeGripperIO, FeedThroughGripperIO, LEDControllerIO) each with Real+Simulated impls selected in System.java switch; lib adds SwerveModuleIO/IMUIO/VisionIO with Real/Sim/Replay variants — half-step above L3 for library-grade reuse across drive and vision, but mechanisms are independent IO classes not a single generic ServoMotorSubsystem base.
rationale.D2: Confirmed System.java holds ArmState enum with requestedArmState/currentArmState fields and ComplexCommands fans goals to elevator+pivot with guarded kinematic sequencing (stow-before-raise, reef-contact zones); this is a real coordinator with intent-execution separation, but no graph/pathfinding logic so capped at L3.
rationale.D3: Confirmed SimulatedElevatorIO/SimulatedPivotIO etc. use custom SimpleMotorSim, SimulatedSwerveModuleIO covers drivetrain, VisionEnvironmentSimulator+SimulatedPhotonVisionIO covers vision in sim, and Robot.java wires WPILOGReader/REPLAY mode with ReplayVisionIO — replay IO variants are present and wired, half-step above L3.
rationale.D4: No src/test directory exists in the robot module; no test Java files found anywhere in the repo; the private CI explicitly skips tests (-x test) on the robot build — scored 0.
rationale.D5: Confirmed @AutoLog on all IO Inputs classes with Logger.processInputs in every subsystem periodic; REAL mode logs to WPILOGWriter+URCL; REPLAY mode wires WPILOGReader; AutoLogOutputManager covers the lib package — half-step above L3 for replay mode being wired, not just present.
rationale.D6: Confirmed Choreo .traj files (Q_pounder_*, Mac_kroket_*) actively referenced via ChoreoTrajectoryAdapter in Autos.java competition routines; PathPlannerTrajectoryGenerator used for on-the-fly auto-align teleop — this is L3 (Choreo optimized + on-the-fly pose driving); no repulsor/dynamic obstacle avoidance to reach L4.
rationale.D7: Confirmed four RealPhotonVisionIO cameras with VisionIO interface (Real/Sim/Replay); addVisionMeasurement wired in SwerveDrive.java with per-camera std-dev tuning (distance^4, ambiguity, multi-tag); TimeInterpolatableBuffer for latency compensation in PhotonVisionIO — half-step above L3 for time-interpolated buffer, but no dedicated RobotState class separating pose from control.
rationale.D8: Confirmed lib/ submodule (com.teamrembrandts) co-shipped in the season repo with its own CI (spotless, checkstyle, unit-tests, Javadoc+GitHub Pages); robot CI has spotless+checkstyle+build on push/PR; README present — half-step above L3 for Javadoc generation and deployment, but library is in-repo not versioned as a separate Maven artifact.
